content: EID diagnosis challenges: unknown pathogen requires unbiased detection methods. Approaches: 1) Metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) from clinical specimens identifies novel pathogens without prior knowledge; 2) Pan-viral/pan-bacterial PCR panels detect broad pathogen classes; 3) Serology for exposure history; 4) Electron microscopy for viral morphology. Biosafety considerations: BSL-3/4 containment for high-consequence pathogens; appropriate PPE; sample inactivation protocols. Pathogen discovery pipeline: sequence → phylogenetic analysis → culture isolation (if possible) → diagnostic assay development → therapeutic/vaccine targets. Genomic epidemiology tracks transmission chains and evolution in real-time. Data sharing critical: GISAID for influenza/SARS-CoV-2 sequences, GenBank for broader pathogen genomics.
